fight. He also said that some Of the
leaders, after their arrest, had ad-
mitted that the Spokane fight was
part of campaign conducted from
the national headquarters, in which
the plan was to overwhelm the Spo-
kane city authorities with men will
ing to go to Jail until the ordinance
against public assemblage on. the
streets was either amended or re-
pealed.


One of the jurors in the case,
William Bass, is colored man. the
first that in years has sat in a jury
in the Spokane. superior court In
the trial of white defendant for a
serious criminal offense. "a By a
strange coincidence, the Industrial
Workers Of the World invites the
colored man to membership on an
equal footing.


The trial of the case may con-
sumo two weeks. Attorney Moore
is being assisted in the case by
Thomas ]. Symmes, sent by the ma
tional headquarters of the I. w. w.
at Chicago. Prosecutor Pugh is be-
ing assisted by John E. Blair, first
assistant corporation counsel. Po-
lice Chief Sullivan and Chief of De-
tectives Burns are appearing as the
complaining witnesses for the state.


The jury in the case was selected
before the adjournment of court
last evening and is composed Of
A. A. Kienholz, teacher, Spokane;
j. HUMPHREYS, farmer, Spokane
Bridge; William Bass, colored.
stone mason, Lidgerwood; ]. L..

south of Cheney; William Johnson,
farmer. d street: George T. Mosier
